content:
Your observation is insightful: as your p99 API latency approaches the physical network round-trip time (RTT) to your nearest datacenter, further latency improvements become increasingly challenging because you hit a fundamental lower bound imposed by physics and infrastructure.

### Key points:
- Your p99 latency dropped from 800ms to ~190ms over a year, a ~25% reduction per quarter.
- If this trend continued linearly, you'd reach sub-millisecond latency next year.
- But network RTT to your datacenter is already on the order of a millisecond or more, so you can't go faster than that for a remote call.

---

## How to architect for sub-millisecond end-to-end latency beyond the network floor

### 1. **Move compute closer to the client (Edge Computing)**
- Deploy services on edge nodes or local points of presence (PoPs) geographically near your users.
